sections.content: Looking at the image, the 6x3 green frame appears to still be to the left of the purple barrier, and the 6x6 frame is still to the right. Let me check the exact positions to see if the 6x3 frame reached its target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check positions after LEFT 4
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        child_colors = [nodes[c]['color'] for c in n['children']]
        print(f"N id={n['id']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, children={child_colors}, hash={n['hash']}")

# Check targets
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['children']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        child_info = [(nodes[c]['color'], nodes[c]['hash']) for c in n['children']]
        print(f"c target id={n['id']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, contains={child_info}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
